Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Jul-20-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Michael Quirk (925 943-6400) for Plaintiffs Eric Broussard and Sementha Hill.
Thomas Crowell of Sidran Law Corp. for Defendant CAPPO Management XL LLC DBA SF Honda and American Finance Corp.
Tara Mohseni for Defendant, Credit Acceptance Corporation.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CREDIT ACCEPTANCE CORPORATION's Motion To Compel Arbitration and stay is granted. As explained in the companion motion of the Dealer, plaintiffs agreed to arbitrate their dispute regarding the Kia under the MVRIC and there is no basis to revoke the arbitration agreement.

Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the adopted ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Cappo Management XL, LLC's ("Dealer") motion to compel arbitration and stay is granted.
